title: The 4 Hidden KPIs Killing Your CPA Firm's Margins
description: Identify and fix the four "lonely growth levers" — unsexy metrics most firms ignore — that silently drain $250K-$1M+ in margin annually.

steps.title: Stop obsessing over top-line growth metrics
steps.content: Most firms track 30+ metrics: new logos, cross-sell rates, utilization targets, WIP backlogs. These are the obvious ones. The firms hitting consistent 20-30% growth are disciplined about four metrics most firms ignore entirely.

steps.title: Track realization at the individual level, weekly
steps.content: Not average realization across the firm. Track it by individual partner, client, and project — weekly, not quarterly. This is where margin silently evaporates. A 3% realization leak across a $10M book is $300K gone, every year.

steps.title: Measure pricing integrity — catch the "adjusters"
steps.content: The partner who quietly reduces invoices to keep a client happy creates a 200-400 basis point margin swing that nobody attributes to pricing erosion. On a $50M firm, that's $1M-$2M left on the table annually. Track every invoice adjustment and who made it.

steps.title: Monitor time-entry timing, not just hours
steps.content: You can't manage utilization if 40% of the firm logs time after Thursday. You're steering on a five-day lag. Late time entry costs the average firm 8-12% in billable hour recovery. That's real revenue loss.

steps.title: Fix invoicing cadence to unlock cash flow
steps.content: Inconsistent billing cycles destroy cash flow, which forces defensive staffing decisions. That's how growth gets capped. Firms with erratic invoicing carry 25-40% higher WIP than necessary — capital stuck in limbo instead of funding expansion.

steps.title: Audit your firm against these four levers
steps.content: Go through each lever and quantify the gap. Most firms find at least one leaking $250K-$1M+ they didn't realize was gone. The fix isn't glamorous, but it's the difference between a firm that grows because it hires and one that grows because it's built to scale.
